mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a strengthening labor market, which boosts aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2008 recovery has been heavily focused on labor market improvement.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 for a continuous 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates past 3%, it signals economic overheating and forces the central bank to consider tightening policy, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting equity valuations.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the inflation trigger is active over a 5-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) and a high unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a severe recessionary shock where both supply capacity and labor demand are falling, leading to sharply negative returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns with greater than 55% accuracy during walk-forward testing in recessionary periods identified by NBER.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate policy (ZIRP) environment, an acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) is a powerful leading indicator of renewed consumer confidence and credit expansion, driving positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS is 0.11, confirming ZIRP regime.
failure_condition: The correlation between HOUST momentum and S&P 500 forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) during the 2009-2015 ZIRP period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong momentum in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and supports positive near-term equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is ~70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: A strategy going long S&P 500 when RSAFS 3-month change is positive and short when negative fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months (the 'Sahm Rule' precursor) signals the onset of a recession, causing a sharp decline in equity prices.
failure_condition: This trigger fails to precede a negative 60-day S&P 500 return in at least 2 of the next 3 NBER-dated recessions.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating but the Fed Funds rate remains low, falling real interest rates create an accommodative environment that boosts asset prices over very short horizons.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6mo change > 0 AND FEDFUNDS 6mo change is flat
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return when the trigger condition is met is not statistically different from the average return when it is not met.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below a trend-like rate signals a broad economic slowdown, reducing forward earnings estimates and causing negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is the broadest measure of economic activity.
failure_condition: In periods following a trigger, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time over a 10-year backtest.
